title: elymus humidus
parts: Elymus humidus has many medicinal parts, such as its rhizomes, roots, leaves, and seeds. The rhizomes and roots of the plant contain bioactive compounds like sesquiterpenoids and phenolic acids, which have been traditionally used to treat inflammatory and gastrointestinal issues. The leaves of Elymus humidus are rich in flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic compounds, which may have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The seeds of the plant contain fatty acids, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds that have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues and boost immune function.
side_effects: Elymus humidus can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal problems, including nausea and vomiting, due to its high content of toxic compounds like glycosides and saponins. In excessive doses, it can also cause allergic reactions, such as skin rashes and respiratory issues. Furthermore, ingesting Elymus humidus can lead to kidney and liver damage, as the plant's toxic compounds can accumulate in the body and cause oxidative stress. In severe cases, improper use of Elymus humidus can even lead to seizures, coma, or even death.
